questions.question: Define e-waste and list any four categories of e-waste.
keyPoints: E-waste is discarded electrical/electronic devices at end-of-life
Categories: Computers/Laptops
Mobile phones
Televisions/Monitors
Home appliances like AC, Refrigerator

questions.question: Explain the 3R principle in the context of e-waste management.
keyPoints: Reduce: Minimize e-waste generation through longer product life
Reuse: Use old electronics for alternate purposes or pass to others
Recycle: Process e-waste to recover valuable materials
Priority order: Reduce > Reuse > Recycle

questions.question: What are the health hazards of improper e-waste disposal?
keyPoints: Lead: Neurological damage, cognitive impairment
Mercury: Kidney damage, Minamata disease
Cadmium: Bone damage, cancer risk
Dioxins: Reproductive issues, immune system damage

questions.question: Explain Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) under E-Waste Rules 2022.
keyPoints: Producers must register with CPCB
Take-back scheme: Collect old products from consumers
Recycling targets set by government
Financial responsibility for collection and recycling

questions.question: Differentiate between formal and informal e-waste sector in India.
keyPoints: Formal: CPCB authorized, proper equipment, safe working conditions
Informal: Unauthorized scrap dealers, manual dismantling, no safety gear
Informal causes pollution and health issues
Formal ensures environmental compliance

questions.question: What is urban mining? Discuss its importance in e-waste management.
keyPoints: Urban mining is recovering valuable materials from e-waste
Extracts gold, silver, copper, rare earth metals
Reduces need for traditional mining
Conserves natural resources and energy

questions.question: List any four hazardous substances found in e-waste and their effects.
keyPoints: Lead: Brain damage, anemia
Mercury: Nervous system damage, kidney failure
Cadmium: Bone damage, lung cancer
Chromium: Respiratory problems, DNA damage
Beryllium: Lung diseases

questions.question: Explain the E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2022 and discuss its key provisions for producers, consumers, and recyclers.
keyPoints: Rules notified in 2022 replacing 2016 rules
Producers: EPR registration, collection targets, take-back scheme
Consumers: Must segregate e-waste, give to authorized collectors
Recyclers: Must obtain CPCB authorization, meet emission standards
Collection mechanism: D2C model, collection centers
Penalties for violation

questions.question: Discuss the environmental and health impacts of improper e-waste disposal. Suggest measures for safe e-waste management at household level.
keyPoints: Environmental: Soil contamination, water pollution, air pollution
Health: Neurological, respiratory, kidney problems, cancer
Household measures: Don't throw e-waste with regular garbage
Identify authorized collectors
Donate reusable electronics
Proper segregation and storage
Support certified recyclers

questions.question: Describe the e-waste recycling process and explain how different materials are recovered. Also discuss the challenges faced by India's e-waste recycling industry.
keyPoints: Process: Collection → Sorting → Dismantling → Shredding → Magnetic separation → Water separation → Chemical processing
Materials recovered: Metals (Fe, Cu, Al, Au, Ag), plastics, glass
Challenges: Low awareness, informal sector competition, inadequate infrastructure
Import of e-waste, lack of technology, financing issues

questions.question: What is the role of various stakeholders in effective e-waste management? Discuss the responsibilities of producers, consumers, government, and recyclers.
keyPoints: Producers: Design for environment, EPR implementation, take-back
Consumers: Responsible disposal, not mixing with municipal waste
Government: Policy formulation, monitoring, awareness campaigns
Recyclers: Safe processing, authorization, pollution control
Collection agencies: Door-to-door collection, awareness
